description: Persistent Memory Context Engine - autonomous JSON documents that live in user's cloud, understand their own context, and get smarter over time

do: Choose practical solutions over perfect engineering
Use breadth-first development for faster validation (36-hour smoke test)
Design for data sovereignty and user control as non-negotiable
Position as infrastructure/middleware, not end-user application
Don't dump all memory at once - use progressive context revelation
Route queries to cheapest capable model (AI arbitrage)
Use Foundation Sprint methodology post-MVP validation, not pre-validation
Implement answer contract validation to eliminate debugging headaches
Position as memory-as-infrastructure, not competing with AI providers
Create 'sandbox' for AI collaboration with user data sovereignty
Use breadth-first development: 1 week to testable MVP vs 6 weeks depth-first
Leverage existing cloud provider sync mechanisms over custom backend
Prove core value with 10-20 test users before enterprise features
Use adaptive thresholds for subject change detection (0.6-0.85 range)
Implement memory weighting: 70% recency, 20% frequency, 10% importance
Allocate context budget: 500 profile, 2048 pinned, 3000 snippets, 452 buffer
Enable strict JSON schema validation with semantic consistency checks
Track entity mentions and cross-reference related decisions
Use REF → extract → ARCHV lifecycle for all mining artifacts
When user says 'mine/scrape/transport', generate artifact immediately without extra commentary
Document external-LLM vs Windsurf agent split in usage instructions
Enforce response format and deviation thresholds
Explore team collaboration workflows with workspace isolation constraints
Document multi-workspace architecture for enterprise PMCE deployment
Validate Cascade capabilities before designing team workflows
Test cross-workspace file reading and Cascade chat storage access
Link physical objects to persistent memory entries via serialized identity
Create digital twin tokens connecting tangible artifacts to 8825 memory capsules
Enable NFC/QR/serial-based access to stored memory contexts
Apply decision matrix before asking user questions (intent × stakes × efficiency)
Proceed with confidence ≥0.7, ask only if confidence <0.5 or safety requires
Use default options between 0.5-0.7 confidence range
State choice + rationale in 1-2 lines when self-resolving decisions
Log decision choices with confidence scores for loop detection
Use chokidar-based Node watchers with awaitWriteFinish and brief debounce
Validate JSON before copying to prevent broken artifacts in - REF -
Write atomically using temporary files; create timestamped backups before overwriting
Respect meta.core_sync_rules.synchronized_sections for governed propagation
Run via LaunchAgents to start at login and keep alive
Prefer absolute paths; log to /tmp/*.log for troubleshooting
dont: Design team workflows before validating individual user value
Assume Cascade capabilities without systematic testing
Build complex sync protocols before validation
Don't rely on Shortcuts for core workflows
Don't overwrite differing files in - REF - without suffixing a timestamp
Don't sync context-specific sections (authoritative_sources, quarantine_rules, meta.context)
framing: Memory as inevitable, not intentional (zero maintenance butler)

lexicon.definition: Persistent Memory Context Engine - autonomous JSON documents that live in user's cloud, understand their own context, and get smarter over time
examples: Dual-layer AI system with intelligence layer (gpt-4o-mini for routing/search) + response layer (main model)
Self-modifying JSON configuration serving as both spec and memory container
Cloud folder storage for multi-device sync with user data sovereignty

lexicon.definition: Positioning AI memory as middleware/infrastructure layer rather than end-user feature; creates interoperability between AI systems
examples: Swiss bank account for AI memory - neutral, private, yours
Not competing with ChatGPT/Claude, creating sandbox for AI collaboration

lexicon.definition: Design philosophy where memory becomes inevitable rather than intentional; contrast to manual knowledge management tools
examples: vs Obsidian: butler (automatic) vs filing cabinet (manual)
Compounding value through personal language modeling over time

lexicon.definition: MVP validation strategy: build minimally testable prototype in 36 hours to validate core value proposition with 2-3 users before full build
examples: Breadth-first over depth-first development
Speed to market validation before 155-220 hour full MVP

lexicon.definition: 20-line Node.js helper that validates AI responses against required schema, eliminating debugging headaches
examples: Enforces refs_used trailers in responses
Validates JSON structure before persistence
Two-call pipeline: Answer → Summary → Validate → Persist

lexicon.definition: Don't dump all memory at once; use confidence check → confirm → load full context approach
examples: Prevents overwhelming AI with irrelevant context
User controls when to load full memory
Intelligent context budgeting within 6000 token limit

lexicon.definition: Three context strategies: focused (single project), exploratory (blend topics), fresh (no memory)
examples: Focused: lock context to single project for deep work
Exploratory: blend multiple related topics for discovery
Fresh: no memory injection for clean slate thinking

lexicon.definition: Route queries to cheapest capable model to optimize cost while maintaining quality
examples: Simple queries → gpt-4o-mini
Complex analysis → gpt-4
Further cost reduction beyond dual-layer architecture

lexicon.definition: Predictively load relevant context while user types to make system feel instantaneous
examples: Background loading based on typing patterns
Cache warming for likely next queries
Seamless user experience with zero perceived latency

lexicon.definition: Memory becomes inevitable rather than intentional, contrasting with manual knowledge management tools
examples: vs Obsidian: butler (automatic) vs filing cabinet (manual)
Compounding value through personal language modeling over time
Lock-in through integration (browser history, calendars, dev environments)

lexicon.definition: Position as middleware/infrastructure layer rather than competing directly with AI providers
examples: Swiss bank account for AI memory - neutral, private, yours
Creating sandbox for AI collaboration and interoperability
Not competing with ChatGPT/Claude, enabling them to work together

lexicon.definition: 36-hour user-testable prototype to validate core value before full development
examples: Basic memory persistence and simple search
Breadth-first over depth-first development approach
1 week to testable vs 6 weeks to complete feature

lexicon.definition: Adaptive algorithm detecting topic shifts using embedding similarity, time gaps, and explicit signals
examples: Embedding cosine similarity threshold 0.72 (adaptive 0.6-0.85)
Time gap detection: 45 minutes between messages
Explicit signals: 'new topic', 'switching gears', 'different subject'

lexicon.definition: Scoring system balancing recency (70%), access frequency (20%), and importance (10%) with decay
examples: 30-day half-life decay with 0.1 minimum retention score
Recent items weighted more heavily than old frequent items
Importance scores manually assigned or ML-derived

lexicon.definition: Token budget management across different context types within 6000 token limit
examples: Project profile: 500 tokens (core identity)
Pinned facts: 2048 tokens (critical context)
Retrieved snippets: 3000 tokens (dynamic context)
Conversation buffer: 452 tokens (recent exchanges)

lexicon.definition: Strict JSON schema enforcement with semantic consistency and contradiction detection
examples: Required fields: conversation_summary, key_conclusions, decisions_made
Contradiction threshold: 0.8 confidence for flagging conflicts
Minimum confidence: 0.6 for acceptance, flag below for review

lexicon.definition: Automated identification and tracking of people, projects, features, deadlines, and metrics
examples: Types: person, project, feature, deadline, metric
Minimum confidence: 0.7 for extraction
Cross-reference strength: strong (0.8), medium (0.6), weak (0.4)

lexicon.definition: Trigger alerts when format/operation/logging fall below set percentages
examples: Format deviation threshold: 0.3 (30% non-compliance triggers alert)
Operation deviation threshold: 0.2 (20% manual code insertion triggers alert)
Logging deviation threshold: 0.1 (10% missing interaction logs triggers alert)

lexicon.definition: Physical artifact with unique identifier linking to specific PMCE memory entry
examples: NFC-enabled token opening dedicated memory capsule
QR-coded object launching specific 8825 context page
Serial-numbered artifact with corresponding digital twin entry

lexicon.definition: Framework preventing user-induced clarification loops by enabling autonomous decision-making with confidence thresholds
examples: Confidence ≥0.7: proceed and state choice + rationale
Confidence 0.5-0.7: use default option from catalog
Confidence <0.5: ask one crisp question with proposed default
Loop detection tracks turns-to-decision and override rates

lexicon.definition: Decision boundaries determining when to proceed autonomously vs ask for clarification
examples: Proceed threshold: 0.7 (high confidence in intent inference)
Ask threshold: 0.5 (low confidence requires user input)
Default range: 0.5-0.7 (use pre-defined defaults from catalog)
Evaluation targets: loop rate ≤5%, avg turns to decision ≤2

lexicon.definition: Pre-defined default choices for common decision types to enable autonomous operation
examples: Research mode default: Tier2_StructuredReview
Learning mode default: Standard
Investor voice general: StrategicButWarm
Investor voice Sequoia: FounderAsSystemsArchitect

lexicon.definition: Background watcher that copies valid JSON from Downloads into - REF - with safe writes and dedupe
lexicon.implementation: Node.js + chokidar with atomic writes and timestamped conflict resolution

lexicon.definition: Watcher that mirrors only synchronized sections from primary 8825 core into sibling cores, preserving context-specific data
Loop that waits for file size to stop changing before reading to avoid partial copies
Write to .tmp file then rename to final path to avoid corruption
If destination exists and differs, append ISO-like timestamp to filename instead of overwriting

summary_log.topic: Project 8825 - Persistent Memory Context Engine (PMCE) development
summary_log.notes: Evolved from initial AI comparison through complete technical architecture to strategic positioning. Key innovations: dual-layer AI system (42% cost savings), self-modifying JSON configuration, cloud folder storage with user data sovereignty, memory-as-infrastructure positioning. MVP timeline: 36-hour smoke test, 155-220 hours complete build. Joju fork proved portability with ~600 lines of domain-specific code. Core philosophy: practical over perfect, breadth-first development, 'zero maintenance butler' vs manual knowledge management.

summary_log.topic: Core routing + sidecar plan (authoritative mapping to 8825_pmce)
summary_log.notes: Core updated with explicit_routing so all '8825/PMCE/memory system/intent/roadmap' references route to projects/8825_pmce.json. Added change_policies to consult PMCE before edits and append to summary_log. Planned Node sidecar CLIs (extract-ref, validate-library, watch-ref) and Ajv schemas for validation.

summary_log.topic: File watcher automation for REF intake and core sync
summary_log.notes: Added automation infrastructure to replace Shortcuts with Node.js watchers. REF Intake Watcher: auto-copies valid JSON from Downloads to - REF - with validation, atomic writes, timestamped dedupe. Core Sync Watcher: auto-propagates synchronized sections from 8825_01-core.json to sibling cores while preserving context-specific sections. Both run via LaunchAgents for persistence. Aligns with zero_maintenance_butler principle and supports REF → extract → ARCHV lifecycle.

summary_log.topic: Technical architecture extraction from archived planning sessions
summary_log.notes: Extracted key technical concepts from archived files: progressive context revelation (confidence check → confirm → load), memory modes (focused/exploratory/fresh), AI arbitrage routing (cheapest capable model), ambient context preloading (predictive loading), answer contract validation (20-line Node.js helper), template system architecture (Outlook/Word/Plain with placeholders and fallbacks). Foundation Sprint methodology positioned post-MVP. Two-call pipeline: Answer → Summary → Validate → Persist. Context budget management within 6000 token limit.

summary_log.topic: Strategic positioning and development methodology extraction
summary_log.notes: Extracted strategic concepts from comprehensive chat mining report: zero maintenance butler philosophy (inevitable vs intentional memory), memory-as-infrastructure positioning (middleware not competitor), smoke test MVP approach (36-hour validation), breadth-first development strategy, cloud provider sync leverage, and user data sovereignty principles. Focused on technical architecture and positioning while excluding financial projections.

Extracted comprehensive technical architecture: subject change detection with adaptive thresholds (0.6-0.85), memory weighting algorithms (70% recency, 20% frequency, 10% importance), context allocation budgeting (6000 token limit across 4 categories), validation schemas with semantic consistency checks, entity extraction with confidence scoring, and redirection rules for sensitive data. Added monitoring and health check frameworks.

Updated with external-LLM vs Windsurf agent split documentation, REF → extract → ARCHV lifecycle enforcement, deviation threshold monitoring, and immediate artifact generation patterns. Added dev backlog item for interaction logging metrics dashboard integration.

summary_log.topic: Physical-digital integration patterns from MTRL TrustyBits collaboration
summary_log.notes: Added patterns for linking physical objects to PMCE memory entries through serialized identity. Explored digital twin tokens that connect tangible artifacts to persistent memory capsules via NFC/QR/serial access methods. Extends PMCE concept to physical-digital continuity and portable memory embodiment.

Implemented autonomous decision-making framework with confidence thresholds to prevent user clarification loops. Key components: decision matrix (intent × stakes × efficiency), confidence thresholds (≥0.7 proceed, 0.5-0.7 use defaults, <0.5 ask), defaults catalog for common choices, decision logging with loop detection. Evaluation metrics: loop rate ≤5%, avg turns to decision ≤2, override rate 10-25%, correct default rate ≥70%. Reduces user effort while maintaining agency through override capability.

summary_log.topic: Team collaboration and enterprise workflow exploration (8825_enterprise_76)
summary_log.notes: Initiated exploration of team workflows for Joju development with PMCE integration. Key discovery: workspace isolation constraint where Cascade conversations in separate workspaces may not cross-communicate. Created sandbox project 8825_enterprise_76 to document multi-workspace architecture options: Shared Dropbox Bridge, Single Unified Workspace, and Cascade History Export. Critical unknowns: Can Cascade access own history? Can Cascade read across workspaces? Need systematic testing of Windsurf capabilities before designing team workflow.

sidecar_integration: Implement Node CLIs: bin/extract-ref, bin/validate-library, bin/watch-ref
Split archived JS into modules: cloudDetector, memoryDb, schemaValidator, metrics, openaiClient, dualLayer
conflict_enforcement: Script to enforce merge_and_conflict_rules on project updates

enhancements.summary: Explicit routing to 8825_pmce; sidecar CLIs planned; schemas to validate library and reports.
